DBT Services Description: Adult and Adolescent Comprehensive Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT). The goal of DBT is to create a life worth living. DBT is a toolbox overflowing with practical skills for managing overwhelming emotions, improving relationships, and navigating life's challenges. It values both acceptance and change. DBT is based on cognitive theory behavioral theory and dialectical philosophies. It was created to treat suicidal and para-suicidal behaviors, and research now shows significant improvement for emotion regulation and impulsivity by becoming more aware of the moment. It helps people experiencing intense emotions, unhealthy coping mechanisms, difficulty regulating emotions and managing stress, and challenges in relationships and communication. DBT typically involves individual therapy and group skills training (mindfulness, distress tolerance, emotional regulation, and interpersonal effectiveness).

DBT Services Description: DBT classes are held once a week for two hours. DBT is a one-year commitment. The Skills phone will be available for coaching 24/7. Participants attend 3-4 orientation sessions with DBT therapists. Attendance is essential, and members will not be allowed to return to the group after four missed sessions.

Services Offered: Individual DBT, Group DBT, Skills Training, Phone Coaching, Consultation
DBT Services Description: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) is a comprehensive cognitive-behavioral treatment for disorders of emotion regulation, including Borderline Personality Disorder. It helps individuals build a life worth living by developing a clearer sense of self, learning healthy management of emotions, encouraging acceptance of life's highs and lows without impulsive action, and creating/improving/maintaining healthy, stable relationships. The program provides standard DBT treatment adherent to Dr. Linehan's model, incorporating Skills Training, Individual Therapy, Phone Coaching, and Consultation.

Full Address (DHS): 9220 Bass Lake Road, Suite 255, New Hope, MN, 55422
Services Offered: Individual DBT, Group DBT, Skills Training
DBT Services Description: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) is an intensive outpatient therapy program for older children, youth, and adults who experience suicidal thinking, self-harm, or other significant behavioral concerns. This therapeutic approach typically lasts six months to a year, or longer if necessary. DBT may be an option for individuals who have not been helped by other therapeutic options, such as traditional psychotherapy (“talk therapy”). Upon referral, an assessment will be conducted to determine if DBT is the best path forward. Vona is deemed an Essential Community Provider and is certified by Minnesota's Department of Human Services under Minnesota Statutes 245i.
